query_normalized: robustness of change‑point detection methods on public financial time series statistics
rationale: The paper studies the same mechanism—change‑point detection methods—using likelihood‑ratio scanning and spectral tests, and evaluates their performance across varied scenarios, providing a methodological baseline that is directly relevant to assessing robustness of such methods on time‑series data (including financial series). This satisfies criterion (a) (same‑mechanism evidence) and (c) (empirical baseline).
